_comment: Shadowrun World Seed Data. Use names as references — the seed script resolves them to IDs automatically.
_todo: TODO: #3 Basic authentication for user access.  Players should be able to create accounts, view their own character sheets and run history, and track their reputation with various factions. They should also be able to see a party overview, showing data known the whole group, but not edit it.  GMs should have additional permissions to manage world data.

organizations.name: Aztechnology
organizations.description: AAA megacorp and de facto ruler of Aztlan. Dominant in consumer goods (Soykaf, Stuffer Shack), pharmaceuticals, military hardware, magical research, and petroleum. Among the most magically powerful corps in the world. Their public face is friendly and colorful; their internal operations are ruthless and rumored to involve blood magic at the highest levels.
organizations.notes:
Jaguar Guards are their elite magical security — typically shamans or physical adepts, always Awakened. Standard security carries assault rifles and wears military-grade armor.

Blood magic connection: credible shadowrunner intel suggests Aztechnology runs ritual sacrifice operations under the guise of magical R&D. Unconfirmed but a major long-term plot hook.

Stuffer Shack convenience stores are an Aztechnology front — useful for surveillance and BTL distribution. They are everywhere in Seattle.

The Aztechnology pyramid in downtown Seattle is a major landmark and extremely hard target. Matrix intrusion into their host has never succeeded by known runners.

Aztlan treaty with UCAS limits their military presence in Seattle, but extraterritoriality means their facilities are sovereign soil. HTR from the pyramid responds to all Aztechnology facilities citywide.

organizations.name: Aztlan
organizations.description: The Aztlaner nation-state formed from most of Mexico and parts of Central America after the Awakening. Effectively a corporate state run by Aztechnology. Has a standing military — the Aztlaner Army — and significant magical resources tied to Aztechnology's research programs.
organizations.notes:
Aztlan and Aztechnology are inseparable. The nation provides political cover and military force; the corp provides funding and direction. Treat them as two arms of the same entity. Aztlaner nationals in Seattle are often Aztechnology assets.

──

organizations.name: Ares Macrotechnology
organizations.description: AAA megacorp based in Detroit. The premier weapons manufacturer and military hardware supplier in the UCAS. Operates across aerospace, firearms, military vehicles, cybernetics, and law enforcement via its Knight Errant Security Services subsidiary. Known for the Ares Predator line of heavy pistols — nearly ubiquitous among shadowrunners.
organizations.notes:
Ares and Aztechnology compete heavily in military hardware and security contracts across the UCAS. Their rivalry is active and occasionally violent at the shadow level — both corps run deniable ops against the other's supply chains and R&D programs.

Knight Errant Security Services is Ares's law enforcement arm and a wholly owned subsidiary. A Knight Errant win on the Seattle city contract is a direct revenue and influence win for Ares corporate. Aurelius is personally invested in the outcome.

Ares vs. Saeder-Krupp is a slower, colder rivalry in aerospace and heavy defense contracts. Lofwyr plays it patiently; Aurelius plays it aggressively. This occasionally creates runner work on both sides.

Razor's Mr. Johnson contact at Ares is a potential recurring employer. Ares gear is ubiquitous, easy to source, and easy to fence — the Predator IV, Alpha, and Light Fire lines are standard issue across the shadows.

──

ltgs.description: Detroit HQ backbone, Seattle access node

organizations.name: Fuchi Industrial Electronics
organizations.description: AAA megacorp and the dominant force in Matrix hardware and cyberdecks worldwide. Fuchi manufactures the most widely used corporate and consumer cyberdecks, as well as telecommunications infrastructure, electronics, and computing systems. The corp is quietly fracturing — a power struggle between the founding Villiers (American) and Shigeda/Nakamura (Japanese) factions is intensifying and will eventually tear the corp apart.
organizations.notes:
Fuchi's internal civil war is the worst-kept secret in corporate shadowrunning. Villiers controls significant North American assets; the Japanese board controls overseas manufacturing. Runners may find themselves hired by one faction to undermine the other — sometimes without knowing it.

Fuchi cyberdecks (the Cyber-5, Cyber-6, and Angst lines) are the standard for mid-tier deckers. Static's Novatech Navigator is a competitor. Fuchi hosts in Seattle are well-defended but fragmented — different divisions maintain different nodes with inconsistent security.

Fuchi has no significant military security force of its own. They contract private security and rely on political leverage.

──

ltgs.description: Villiers faction North America operations host
Tokyo HQ backbone, Seattle access node

ltgs.notes: Whoever controls this node controls the narrative of the Villiers/board split.

organizations.name: Renraku Computer Systems
organizations.description: AAA megacorp based in Chiba, Japanese Imperial State. Dominant in Matrix architecture, computers, robotics, and consumer electronics. Renraku designed and maintains large portions of the global Matrix backbone, giving them surveillance reach that most corps can only envy. In Seattle, their defining presence is the SCIRE — the Renraku Arcology, a massive self-contained city-within-a-city in downtown Seattle.
organizations.notes:
The Renraku Arcology (SCIRE) in downtown Seattle is one of the largest structures on the West Coast — a 100-story self-contained complex housing 90,000+ residents, corporate offices, factories, schools, and hospitals. It is essentially a sovereign city block. Extremely hard target.

Red Samurai are Renraku's elite security force — among the most feared corporate soldiers in the world. Full body armor, milspec weapons, rigger-controlled drone support, and absolute loyalty. They do not take prisoners on extraterritorial soil.

Renraku's Matrix architecture division means they have back-channel access to infrastructure that other corps don't. A decker who crosses Renraku may find their SIN flagged across multiple unrelated hosts.

Knight Errant relationship: Renraku uses Knight Errant for perimeter security around non-Arcology Seattle facilities — a pragmatic arrangement given Ares's military hardware supply relationship with Renraku. This creates an awkward dynamic: KE officers patrol the outside of Renraku buildings while Red Samurai control everything inside the threshold. Jurisdictional disputes are common and occasionally violent.

──

ltgs.description: Chiba HQ backbone, Seattle access node

ltgs.notes: Rumored to contain data from pre-lockdown SCIRE experiments. The rating is not inflated.

organizations.name: Shiawase Corporation
organizations.description: AAA megacorp and the oldest of the Big Eight. Shiawase holds the distinction of winning the first corporate extraterritoriality case in 2001, legally establishing that megacorps are sovereign entities — a ruling that reshaped the entire world. Dominant in nuclear power generation, biotechnology, environmental systems, and munitions. Operates with a deeply traditional Japanese corporate culture (kaisha) emphasizing family and loyalty.
organizations.notes:
Shiawase is the most internally stable of the Big Eight — the Shiawase family retains genuine control, unlike corps fractured by shareholder politics. This makes them harder to manipulate through internal division.

Their biotech and environmental divisions are active in Seattle — wastewater reclamation, toxic site remediation (often in the Barrens), and pharmaceutical research. This gives them legitimate reasons to operate in otherwise low-visibility areas.

Shiawase security (Shiawase Envirotech Security) is professional, quiet, and effective. They rarely appear in shadowrunner stories because they rarely create incidents — they handle problems internally before they escalate.

──

ltgs.description: Osaka HQ backbone, Seattle access node

organizations.name: Saeder-Krupp Heavy Industries
organizations.description: AAA megacorp based in Neu-Essen, Germany. The most financially powerful corporation in the world by most measures. Dominant in heavy industry, aerospace, banking, chemicals, and metallurgy. Controlled by the Great Dragon Lofwyr, who holds a majority stake and serves as CEO — making Saeder-Krupp the only AAA corp openly and directly run by a Great Dragon.
organizations.notes:
Lofwyr is the single most dangerous entity the runners are ever likely to have as an enemy — or employer. He plans on timescales of decades and has intelligence assets woven through every major institution on the continent. He does not make mistakes. If Lofwyr takes an interest in the runners, something very large is in motion.

Saeder-Krupp competes with Ares in aerospace and heavy military contracts. Their rivalry is cold, expensive, and fought primarily through proxies.

Saeder-Krupp's Seattle presence is primarily financial and industrial — they hold significant stakes in Pacific Rim shipping infrastructure, port operations, and a metals processing facility in the industrial district. Low profile, but the money flows through everything.

──

ltgs.description: Essen HQ backbone, Seattle access node

ltgs.notes: If this node is ever accessed, assume Lofwyr knows within minutes. The rating is not a typo.

organizations.name: Mitsuhama Computer Technologies
organizations.description: AAA megacorp based in Kyoto, Japanese Imperial State. Dominant in robotics, computers, heavy machinery, and magical research. MCT has the most aggressive anti-shadowrunner policy of any megacorp — their facilities operate under a standing shoot-on-sight order for unauthorized personnel, earning the designation 'zero zone' in runner slang. Rumored to have been founded with Yakuza capital, a connection both parties deny.
organizations.notes:
Zero zone policy: MCT security has standing authorization to use lethal force against any unauthorized personnel. There is no warning shot. Runners who take MCT jobs need to understand that getting caught is death, and extraction ops against MCT facilities carry a premium for exactly this reason.

MCT's Yakuza connection is an open secret. The Watada-rengo (Seattle Yakuza) have historically given MCT favorable treatment in exchange for financial arrangements that predate the corp's AAA status. This relationship is exploitable as a plot hook.

MCT's magical research division is second only to Aztechnology in scope. They focus on paracritter study, magical industrial applications, and drone-spirit hybrid research that puts them ahead of most competitors in the emerging field of magical automation.

Knight Errant relationship: MCT explicitly refuses Knight Errant contracts for any of their Seattle facilities, citing concerns about Ares corporate espionage risk (and because KE's transparency standards are incompatible with zero-zone operations). MCT runs entirely proprietary security. This has created friction as KE wins more Seattle territory — MCT facilities become islands of non-KE jurisdiction that are occasionally used as a point of contention in the city contract debate.

──

ltgs.notes: Connecting logs your access node. MCT cross-references all visitors against known runner SINs.

ltgs.description: Kyoto HQ backbone, Seattle access node

ltgs.notes: Known only to senior Watada-rengo and MCT board level. Most sensitive data in MCT's Seattle operation.

organizations.name: Yamatetsu Corporation
organizations.description: AAA megacorp, the youngest and most progressive of the Big Eight. Dominant in biotech, cyberware, aquacultural technology, and Pacific Rim shipping and logistics. Notable for having the highest proportion of metahuman employees and executives of any AAA corp — a deliberate policy that has earned both loyalty from metahuman communities and resentment from more traditional Japanese corps.
organizations.notes:
Yamatetsu controls significant portions of Seattle's port and Pacific shipping infrastructure — nearly a third of container traffic through the Port of Seattle runs on Yamatetsu logistics contracts. This gives them enormous economic leverage and a reason to care deeply about stability in the Barrens (where much of their warehouse workforce lives).

Their metahuman-friendly reputation means Yamatetsu occasionally employs shadowrunners openly as 'security consultants' — they're less concerned with corporate optics than their competitors. Metahuman runners may find Yamatetsu a more accessible employer.

Yamatetsu cyberware (particularly their biocompatible 'deltaware-adjacent' lines) is cutting-edge but expensive. Dr. Anand's Seattle lab is rumored to be testing next-generation cultured bioware that has attracted corporate espionage interest from Aztechnology and MCT.

──

ltgs.description: Vladivostok HQ backbone, Seattle access node

organizations.name: Knight Errant Security Services
organizations.description: Wholly owned subsidiary of Ares Macrotechnology and the most serious challenger to Lone Star's dominance in UCAS law enforcement contracting. Knight Errant presents a cleaner, more professional, and explicitly military face compared to Lone Star — better equipped, better trained, and aggressively expanding. They hold corporate security contracts with several Seattle megacorp facilities and are in active pursuit of the Seattle city law enforcement contract, which Lone Star currently holds.
organizations.notes:
Knight Errant vs. Lone Star is the defining law enforcement rivalry in Seattle right now. The city contract is up for renewal and the vote is politically contested — Lone Star has entrenched relationships with city council and UCAS government but Knight Errant has money, equipment, and a corruption-free image on their side.

For runners, Knight Errant is more dangerous than Lone Star in direct confrontation — better gear, better training, military doctrine, and no culture of looking the other way for the right bribe. However, they are also less omnipresent. They currently patrol corporate districts where they hold contracts (Bellevue, Downtown corporate blocks, Ares-adjacent facilities) and have minimal presence in the Barrens.

Knight Errant officers are equipped with Ares milspec: Ares Alpha assault rifles, Ares Predator sidearms, full-body corporate armor, and rigger-supported drone overwatch on high-value patrol routes. HTR teams deploy in armed APCs.

Knight Errant has a strict use-of-force doctrine — everything is documented, body cameras are standard, and excessive force is prosecuted internally. This makes them harder to bribe and harder to provoke into an incident that embarrasses them. They fight clean and they fight to win.

If Knight Errant wins the city contract, Lone Star loses nearly all legal authority in Seattle. The transition period would be chaotic — a window of opportunity or danger for runners depending on how they play it.

Knight Errant actively recruits ex-military and ex-Ares security. Several of their Seattle officers have combat zone deployments. Do not mistake them for beat cops.

──

ltgs.description: Chicago HQ backbone, Seattle access node

ltgs.notes: Contains KE's compiled Lone Star corruption dossier for the city contract bid. Valuable to both sides.

organizations.name: Lone Star Security
organizations.description: Seattle's contracted law enforcement. Entrenched, corrupt, and under serious threat. Lone Star has held the Seattle law enforcement contract for years, but Knight Errant's bid has forced a performance review that is exposing how badly the organization has deteriorated at its upper levels. On the street, individual officers range from genuinely dedicated to openly on the take.
organizations.notes:
Heat level rises when runners draw too much attention downtown. Lone Star response times vary wildly by district — fast in Downtown and Bellevue, slow in the Sprawl, nonexistent in the deepest Barrens.

Corruption is systemic at the command level. Chief Hargrove has relationships with at least two organized crime groups and takes a cut of vice enforcement revenue. Mid-level officers know not to ask questions. Beat cops in the Barrens are either idealists or they've stopped caring.

The contract renewal vote is in 6 months. Hargrove is in full political survival mode — he is calling in every favor, leaking damaging information about Knight Errant whenever possible, and has quietly authorized some very dirty operations to make the city look like it needs Lone Star's institutional knowledge rather than KE's military polish.

Runners who know about Hargrove's corruption have leverage. Runners who make that leverage visible will find Lone Star becomes very interested in them very quickly.

Lone Star Special Investigations (Captain Ferris) is the one division that is genuinely effective and not obviously dirty. They handle corporate crime and organized crime cases and are not in Hargrove's pocket — yet.

──

ltgs.description: Public host, warrant lookups & civilian reports

ltgs.notes: Static's backdoor enters here. She has read access to patrol assignments and BOLO alerts.
Contains documentation of Hargrove's organized crime relationships. Most dangerous data in Lone Star's possession.

organizations.name: Yakuza (Watada-rengo)
organizations.description: The dominant organized crime organization in Seattle, and one of the most powerful Yakuza families in North America. The Watada-rengo controls the majority of Seattle's vice economy — gambling, BTL distribution, prostitution, loan sharking, and protection — primarily in Japanese and metahuman communities. Operates with a strict code of honor (ninkyo) that distinguishes it from western crime organizations and makes it both more predictable and more dangerous.

leadership.title: Saiko-komon (Senior Advisor)
leadership.name: Yamada Kenji — rumored to be the true power behind Shotozumi

organizations.notes:
The Yakuza-MCT financial relationship predates MCT's AAA status. The Watada-rengo provided seed capital during MCT's early expansion in exchange for favorable distribution deals and a permanent cut of Seattle operations. Both parties deny this publicly and violently.

Yakuza territory in Seattle is well-defined and enforced. Crossing into their vice operations without permission or a cut is how runners end up in Puget Sound. However, the Watada-rengo occasionally contracts shadowrunners for deniable work — particularly against Seoulpa Ring operations and corporate espionage targets.

The code of ninkyo means the Yakuza honor their contracts — if they hire you, you get paid. If you steal from them or burn them, the response is methodical and permanent. They do not get emotional; they get even, on their own timeline.

Seoulpa Ring encroachment is the Watada-rengo's primary strategic problem right now. Shotozumi is deploying significant resources to contain it, which means the organization is stretched and may be vulnerable to outside pressure in ways it normally would not be.

──

ltgs.description: Shotozumi Ramen, Little Tokyo — known front
Pacific Rim Import/Export LLC — known front
Watada Investments Group — shadow community knows this one

organizations.name: Seattle Mafia
organizations.description: The Seattle family of the Italian-American Mafia, a member of the national Commissione. Once dominant in Seattle organized crime, the Mafia has been in slow decline for decades — squeezed by the Yakuza's expansion, the rise of the Seoulpa Rings, and internal leadership instability. They remain a significant force in traditional vice markets and hold deep political connections accumulated over generations, but they are no longer the apex predator they once were.

leadership.title: Consigliere
leadership.name: Father Vincent Argento — a Catholic priest who has served three dons

organizations.notes:
The Mafia's greatest remaining asset is their political depth — decades of favors, blackmail material, and relationships with judges, city council members, and UCAS federal officials. Even in decline, crossing the Seattle family means potentially waking up to find your SIN flagged, your landlord evicted you, and your banking account frozen.

They control significant Tacoma port operations and have deep roots in the longshoreman and dock worker unions. Any cargo running through Tacoma that isn't Yakuza or corp-controlled likely has Mafia involvement.

Bigio is pragmatic and tired. He's been fighting the Yakuza for 20 years and knows the Mafia is losing. He's quietly exploring whether there's a sustainable arrangement to be made — which his younger capos view as weakness and the Yakuza view as an opportunity to finish the job.

The Mafia will hire shadowrunners, but they pay less than the Yakuza and are more likely to use runners as a throwaway asset when things go wrong.

──

ltgs.description: Bigio Family Imports LLC — known front
Argento Legal Group — money laundering vehicle

ltgs.notes: Contains decades of compromising material on city officials. Father Argento is the only other party with access.

organizations.name: Red Dragon Association
organizations.description: The dominant Triad organization operating in Seattle, with roots in Hong Kong and the Canton region. The Red Dragon Association controls criminal operations in the Chinatown district and significant portions of the International District, specializing in smuggling, counterfeiting, BTL production, and Matrix-based financial crime. More pragmatic and commercially minded than the Yakuza — the Red Dragon makes deals with anyone who can pay.

leadership.title: Dragon Head (Shan Chu)
leadership.name: Henry Tsang — publicly a legitimate import/export businessman

leadership.title: Incense Master (Heung Chu)
leadership.name: Lin Bao — oversees ritual initiations and internal discipline

organizations.notes:
The Red Dragon Association is the most willing of the major syndicates to work with outside parties, including shadowrunners and even other criminal groups on a transactional basis. This makes them accessible but also means their loyalty is entirely financial — they will not take personal risks for a business relationship.

Their BTL production operation is sophisticated and largely isolated from street-level distribution, which they subcontract through gangs like the Cutters. The Engineer's lab location is one of the most sought-after pieces of intelligence in the Seattle shadow community.

The Triads and Yakuza maintain a cautious non-aggression arrangement in Seattle — they don't compete directly in most markets and carve up territory pragmatically. This arrangement is under periodic strain and has broken down violently twice in the last decade.

Red Dragon smuggling connections make them the go-to contact for obtaining restricted goods — cyberware, weapons, forbidden tech — without corp or Lone Star involvement. Runners with Triad connections can access a supply chain that circumvents most normal acquisition channels.

──

ltgs.description: Hong Kong Trading Company — known front
Pacific Rim Shipping & Freight — smuggling front

ltgs.notes: BTL lab location is derivable from this node. Most sought-after paydata in Seattle shadow community.

organizations.name: Seoulpa Rings
organizations.description: A loose confederation of Korean-American criminal organizations operating in Seattle, rising rapidly in power and influence. Unlike the Yakuza's strict hierarchy or the Mafia's family structure, the Seoulpa Rings are a decentralized network of semi-independent cells (rings) that cooperate opportunistically. This makes them harder to decapitate and faster to adapt. They have been aggressively challenging Yakuza territory in the Barrens and mid-tier vice markets for the past several years.
organizations.notes:
The Seoulpa Rings do not have a unified command — each ring operates independently, with cooperation arranged through intermediaries. This is their greatest strength: there is no single Seoulpa boss to kill, bribe, or flip. Taking down one ring barely slows the others.

They are expanding into Yakuza territory deliberately and systematically, using cheaper BTL prices and more flexible arrangements to pull street-level distributors away from Yakuza control. The Watada-rengo is responding with violence; the Seoulpa Rings are responding with more violence and faster recruitment.

Seoulpa Rings hire shadowrunners frequently and pay competitively. They're particularly interested in deniable ops against Yakuza infrastructure — warehouse raids, data theft, personnel extraction (or elimination). They do not always tell runners who the target actually belongs to.

The Rings have notable strength in tech crime — matrix fraud, SIN forgery, credstick manipulation. Several ring leaders have former corporate IT backgrounds, which gives them capabilities most street gangs lack.

──

ltgs.description: Seoul House Restaurant — Park Jin-ho ring front
Hangang Electronics LLC — tech crime front, Kim Soo-yeon ring
Kim Soo-yeon ring — tech crime & SIN forgery

ltgs.notes: Compromising this node identifies the Bridge. Yakuza has been trying to locate it for two years.

organizations.name: Humanis Policlub
organizations.description: The largest and most politically influential anti-metahuman organization in the UCAS. Humanis operates on two levels simultaneously: a public-facing political movement advocating for 'human rights' and anti-metahuman legislation, and a covert network that funds and directs violence against orks, trolls, elves, dwarves, and their allies. In Seattle, Humanis has elected officials sympathetic to their cause, established neighborhood chapters, and a militant enforcement arm that operates outside official club structures.

leadership.title: Seattle Chapter President
leadership.name: Councilman Arthur Vogel — publicly moderate, privately funds the enforcement arm

organizations.notes:
Humanis is not a conventional criminal organization and should not be treated as one. They have lawyers, political connections, media spokespeople, and plausible deniability for every act of violence they sponsor. Runners who take anti-Humanis jobs need to expect that the target will have legal resources and political protection, not just muscle.

In Seattle, Humanis has growing influence in Bellevue and some Downtown corporate circles. Their narrative — that metahumans represent unfair competition for human jobs and resources — resonates with economically anxious communities. Do not underestimate their political reach.

The enforcement arm (the 'Policlub Rangers' in internal documents, never publicly) carries out beatings, arson, and killings against metahumans and their advocates. They are careful, use deniable assets, and have never been successfully prosecuted because Councilman Vogel sits on the public safety committee that oversees Lone Star.

Metahuman runner characters may find Humanis a deeply personal antagonist. Human runner characters may be approached for work by Humanis-adjacent Mr. Johnsons — the work will seem neutral until it isn't.

──

ltgs.description: Councilman Vogel's constituent office — officially separate from Humanis
Deacon's command node — enforcement orders & target lists
ltgs.notes: Contains authorization chains linking Vogel directly to enforcement violence. Career-ending paydata.

organizations.name: Halloweeners
organizations.description: One of the most notorious and feared gangs in the Seattle Barrens — not for their organization or numbers, but for their absolute unpredictability and love of fire. The Halloweeners wear orange and black, favor grotesque Halloween masks and face paint, and treat arson as both a tactical tool and a form of entertainment. Active primarily in the Redmond Barrens and Puyallup district.
organizations.notes:
The Halloweeners do not negotiate. They do not take protection money and leave you alone. If they decide your block is interesting, they will burn it and move on. This makes them useless as an employer and dangerous as an obstacle.

Their territory overlaps with the Crimson Crush in Redmond, which generates constant low-level warfare. The Halloweeners use fire as their primary tactical advantage — the Crush has numbers and organization but cannot always respond fast enough to an arson attack in a densely packed Barrens neighborhood.

Runners who need a distraction can sometimes point the Halloweeners at a target by leaving the right provocation — graffiti tags, a visible enemy presence, anything that draws their attention. This is not controllable and will cause collateral damage.

Some Halloweeners are genuinely pyrokinetically Awakened, which makes them extremely dangerous. Unconfirmed but consistent enough in runner reports to take seriously.

organizations.name: Crimson Crush
organizations.description: The largest predominantly-ork gang in the Redmond Barrens and one of the most organized street gangs in Seattle. The Crimson Crush controls significant territory in Redmond through a combination of size, discipline, and genuine community investment — they provide protection to ork and troll families in their territory in a way that Lone Star never has. More structured than most gangs, with defined ranks and a functioning internal economy.

leadership.title: Boss
leadership.name: Red Sonja (human woman, earned the position through demonstrated brutality and tactical skill)

leadership.title: Logistics
leadership.name: Mama Crushka — runs the community support network that keeps civilian loyalty

organizations.notes:
The Crimson Crush occupies an unusual position in the Barrens — they are simultaneously a predatory criminal organization and a genuine community institution. In their core territory, they fund food distribution, protect metahuman families from Humanis violence, and provide employment. This community support makes them nearly impossible to displace through conventional law enforcement pressure.

They will hire shadowrunners, particularly for operations that require skills their membership doesn't have (decking, magic, vehicle work). They pay fairly by Barrens standards and honor their arrangements.

Red Sonja's leadership is contested internally — several ork lieutenants believe the boss should be ork. She has survived two assassination attempts in the last year and responds to challenges with public violence. The internal tension is exploitable.

Conflict with the Halloweeners is ongoing and low-level. Conflict with the Spikes flares periodically over borderland territory.

organizations.name: Ancients
organizations.description: An exclusively elf go-gang with chapters across the UCAS, making them one of the few street gangs that operates at anything approaching a regional scale. The Ancients wear green and silver, ride high-performance motorcycles, and maintain a level of discipline and organization that blurs the line between street gang and criminal organization. They do courier work, smuggling, and contract muscle for organized crime groups willing to pay their rates.

leadership.title: National Gang Leader
leadership.name: Green Lucifer — based in Chicago, sets overall policy

organizations.notes:
The Ancients are unique among Seattle gangs in that they actively cultivate relationships with criminal organizations and corporations for contract work. They function partly as a gang and partly as a mercenary courier and muscle service. Their elf-only membership gives them a cultural cohesion most gangs lack — and an insularity that makes them hard to infiltrate.

They control significant smuggling routes along the Puyallup/Auburn corridor and have connections into Salish-Shidhe territory for cross-border work. A runner who needs to move something quietly across the Salish border should know the Ancients.

The Ancients have an unspoken arrangement with the Yakuza for courier work — not an alliance, but a recurring business relationship. They are careful not to become dependent on any one organization.

Non-elf runners will never be formally welcomed by the Ancients, but they can do business. Elf runners may be actively recruited.

organizations.name: Spikes
organizations.description: A predominantly-troll gang operating in the Redmond Barrens and adjacent areas. The Spikes are smaller than the Crimson Crush but individually more physically dangerous — a troll gang in a straight fight is a different problem than most gangs. They are brutal, territorial, and not particularly organized beyond a core leadership. They control several blocks in northern Redmond through pure physical intimidation.
organizations.notes:
The Spikes are not subtle and not strategic. They hold territory because challenging trolls in their home turf is a proposition most gangs choose to avoid. They are useful as a blunt instrument — if you need something wrecked or someone intimidated, they are available for credsticks or BTL.

They are not reliable for anything requiring planning, discretion, or follow-through. Do not hire them for jobs that require all three of a plan, secrecy, and a specific outcome.

Conflict with the Crimson Crush is ongoing and territorial. The Crush has numbers; the Spikes have trolls. Neither side has been able to decisively end the other.

organizations.name: 405 Hellhounds
organizations.description: A vehicle and go-gang controlling sections of the Interstate 405 corridor south of Seattle and into the Renton sprawl. The Hellhounds make their money through highway robbery, cargo theft from commercial vehicles, and extortion of businesses that rely on I-405 traffic. They are fast, mobile, and operate in mixed terrain — equally comfortable on the highway and in the urban fringe.

leadership.title: Mechanic / Tech
leadership.name: Wrench — keeps the fleet running, occasional drone operator

organizations.notes:
The Hellhounds are primarily useful to runners as either an obstacle (for cargo jobs using I-405) or as hired mobility — they know the roads, have vehicles, and can move people or goods across the corridor quickly.

They have a loose arrangement with the Ancients on non-overlapping smuggling routes and occasionally do subcontracted courier work when the Ancients are overbooked.

Lone Star has a standing task force targeting Hellhound highway operations but makes little progress — the gang disperses fast and the I-405 corridor spans multiple jurisdictions. Knight Errant has not yet established presence along the corridor.

organizations.name: First Nation
organizations.description: A Native American street gang and political movement occupying contested space between criminal organization and activist group. First Nation operates in the border zones between Seattle proper and Salish-Shidhe Council territory, advocating (sometimes violently) for Native American rights, land reclamation, and resistance to corporate encroachment on traditional territories. Their criminal operations fund political activities; their political activities provide cover and recruits for criminal operations.

leadership.title: Spiritual Advisor
leadership.name: Elder Mary Whitehorse — a genuine shaman whose involvement legitimizes the movement to the broader Native community

organizations.notes:
First Nation is complicated. Some members are genuine political activists with real grievances; others are opportunistic criminals using the movement as cover. Strongbow holds both factions together through force of personality and a genuine political vision that, whether you agree with it or not, is coherent and consistent.

They have quiet connections to the Salish-Shidhe Council government — not formal recognition, but intelligence-sharing and occasional material support. First Nation operatives can cross the Salish border more easily than most runners.

Runners hired by First Nation often discover the job has more political meaning than the briefing suggested. They are not dishonest employers, but they have an agenda that extends beyond any single job.

They are explicitly hostile to Aztechnology, MCT, and any corp actively pursuing development in traditionally Native territories near Seattle. They have carried out multiple sabotage operations against corporate survey and construction teams.

organizations.name: Rusted Stilettos
organizations.description: A mixed-metahuman gang operating in the Redmond Barrens, notable for their use of discarded and jury-rigged cyberware — limbs, eyes, and reflex boosters stripped from corpsec casualties, medical waste, and shadowrunner gear fenced into the Barrens. The Stilettos are not large, but individual members punch significantly above their tier due to their patchwork augmentations.

leadership.title: Boss
leadership.name: Razormouth — has a jaw full of implanted blades of unclear origin

leadership.title: Tech / Ripperdoc
leadership.name: Patches — installs the gang's cyberware, sometimes without anesthesia, sometimes without full functionality

organizations.notes:
The Stilettos are buyers of black-market and stolen cyberware — if a runner needs to fence augmentation-related gear quickly and quietly in the Barrens, the Stilettos will pay cash with no questions about provenance.

Patches operates a back-alley ripperdoc clinic that is simultaneously the gang's greatest asset and liability. The clinic attracts desperate people from across the Barrens — customers who have nowhere else to go for cybernetic maintenance or emergency surgery. This gives the Stilettos unusual community intelligence.

The gang's reliance on degraded, mismatched cyberware means essence costs among leadership are extreme. Several senior members are approaching burnout, which is creating succession anxiety and short-term thinking at the top.

organizations.name: The Cutters
organizations.description: Mid-level gang controlling parts of the Redmond Barrens. Run protection rackets and serve as mid-level distributors for BTL chips, some of which flow through Stuffer Shack supply lines.
organizations.notes: Useful muscle for hire if standing is high enough. Unknowingly tied into Aztechnology's BTL distribution network via Stuffer Shack — they think they're independent. They are not.

locations.name: Dante's Inferno
locations.description: Multi-level nightclub with a hell theme. Popular with shadowrunners and corporate suits alike.
locations.notes: Good place to meet fixers. Avoid the upper floors — Yakuza territory.

locations.name: The Rusty Nail
locations.description: Grimy dive bar. No questions asked, cheap synthahol.

locations.name: Aztechnology Research Campus
locations.description: High-security R&D campus. Magical wards, HTR teams, dual-natured guards. Primary site for magical research in the Pacific Northwest division.
locations.notes: HTR response time ~4 minutes. Mana barriers on all labs. Dr. Quijada's primary workspace. Sub-basement levels are rumored to house blood magic ritual spaces — unconfirmed.

locations.name: Aztechnology Pyramid
locations.description: Aztechnology's Seattle regional headquarters. A stepped pyramid tower that dominates the downtown skyline. Houses executive offices, a host node rated among the most secure in the city, Jaguar Guard barracks, and a public-facing corporate gallery and Soykaf cafe on the ground floor.
locations.notes: Extraterritorial — Aztlan sovereign soil. Jaguar Guards patrol all floors. The public ground floor is safe; anything above level 3 requires corporate ID. The host is considered a black IC deathtrap by every decker who's discussed it. HTR launches from here for all Seattle Aztechnology sites.

locations.name: Stuffer Shack - Redmond Barrens
locations.description: One of dozens of Aztechnology-owned Stuffer Shack franchise locations scattered across Seattle. 24-hour, sells cheap soykaf, nutrisoy, and BTL chips under the counter.
locations.notes: Surveillance cameras feed back to an Aztechnology monitoring node. The night manager, a troll named Gus, supplements income by moving BTL chips sourced through the Cutters. He does not know who actually owns the franchise.

locations.name: Stuffer Shack - Downtown
locations.description: Higher-end Stuffer Shack location near the corporate towers. Cleaner, better stocked, but the surveillance is tighter.
locations.notes: Corporate foot traffic means this location gets audited more frequently. Not a safe place for runners to linger. The host node here has a direct uplink to the Pyramid.

locations.name: Club Penumbra
locations.description: One of the most famous shadowrunner-friendly establishments in Seattle. Club Penumbra occupies a converted waterfront warehouse and runs three floors of live music, dancing, and private booths with Matrix terminals. It operates as genuine neutral ground — corp suits, runners, gangers, fixers, and syndicate representatives all drink here, and the management enforces the peace with professional security that has no loyalties to any faction.
locations.notes:
Penumbra's neutrality is its product. The owner, a human woman known only as Doris, has maintained independence from every faction that has tried to claim the club through a combination of leverage, paid-for protection, and a security team that is unusually well-equipped for a nightclub.

The Matrix terminals in the private booths are hardened against passive surveillance and are the de facto standard meeting infrastructure for runners who need a secure channel in meat space. Do not assume they are completely clean — they are safer than most alternatives, not safe.

Fixers use the booths for introductions. Mr. Johnsons use them for initial contact. Syndicate intermediaries use them for cross-faction negotiation. If something shadow-related is happening in Seattle, someone in this building probably knows about it.

The live music is legitimately good. The cover is 20 nuyen and the synthahol is overpriced. Both are worth it.

locations.name: Underworld 93
locations.description: An underground club in the literal sense — built into a network of converted utility tunnels and maintenance spaces beneath Downtown Seattle. Underworld 93 is darker, louder, and less curated than Club Penumbra. The clientele skews toward the harder end of the shadow community: gangers, smugglers, BTL dealers, and runners who prefer their meeting spots to not have security cameras.
locations.notes:
Underworld 93 has no visible ownership. The staff rotate constantly and none of them will give you a name. There are four known entrances, two of which are through adjacent buildings whose owners claim ignorance. Lone Star has raided the club three times; each time it was empty an hour before they arrived.

BTL chips are openly traded in the back corridors. Stolen cyberware, weapons, and forged SINs can be sourced here with minimal effort. If you need something quickly and don't want to explain yourself to a fixer, Underworld 93 is the market.

There are persistent rumors of a fifth sub-level that doesn't appear on any building schematic, used for high-value exchanges that require even less visibility than the main club. No runner interviewed has claimed to have personally accessed it — only heard about it from someone who knew someone.

locations.name: The Jackal's Lantern
locations.description: A runner and low-life bar in the volcanic Puyallup district, built into the reinforced lower level of a building that has survived three nearby lava flow events through sheer construction stubbornness. The Jackal's Lantern is the primary runner watering hole for the southern Barrens — rougher than Club Penumbra, cheaper than Dante's, and completely disinterested in your business unless your business is causing problems.
locations.notes:
The bar is run by a dwarf named Cutter MacAllister who has tended bar here for fifteen years and will continue to do so until either the volcano or someone's bullet ends the arrangement. He hears everything, repeats nothing without payment, and pours generous measures.

Puyallup's volcanic activity means ash falls are common and the air outside carries particulate. The Lantern's filtered interior is one reason people stay longer than they planned. The bar stocks respirators behind the counter for customers who arrive underprepared.

Helloweener territory begins three blocks east. MacAllister has a long-standing informal arrangement with the gang — they don't come inside, and he doesn't ask where the credsticks his regulars spend came from. It is an arrangement that has held for seven years and will eventually not hold.

locations.name: Renraku Arcology (SCIRE)
locations.description: The largest single structure in the Pacific Northwest — a 100-story stepped arcology tower housing approximately 90,000 residents, Renraku's Seattle corporate headquarters, manufacturing floors, schools, hospitals, shopping centers, a full agricultural level, and its own power generation. The SCIRE is functionally a self-contained city inside Seattle's city limits. It is extraterritorial Renraku sovereign space from the parking structure to the roof.
locations.notes:
Entry to the public-accessible lower levels (floors 1-3) requires a valid SIN scan and biometric registration. Everything above floor 3 requires Renraku corporate credentials. The residential levels are accessible only to registered residents and their logged guests. The upper corporate floors are Red Samurai territory.

The SCIRE has its own Matrix host architecture — a fully internal grid that is hardened against external intrusion and maintained separately from the public Seattle grid. Static's assessment: the host is extremely dangerous and she will not jack into it without significant preparation and a very good reason.

For scale: the SCIRE's internal population is larger than many UCAS cities. It has its own transit system, internal law enforcement (Renraku Security, separate from Red Samurai), and a judicial system. A person could theoretically never leave and have every material need met.

Runner operations targeting the SCIRE require treating it as a full infiltration of a sovereign corporate state, not a building. Floor plans above level 10 are not available on any public or shadow market source at any confirmed price.

locations.name: Touristville
locations.description: The commercial strip on the outer edge of the Redmond Barrens — close enough to the sprawl to feel dangerous, far enough from the deep Barrens to be survivable for outsiders. Touristville exists to sell the Barrens experience to people who don't actually live there: cheap bars, black market stalls, shadow services brokers, and enough controlled grit to satisfy the curiosity of corporate wage slaves who want a story to tell on Monday. Locals regard it with a mixture of economic dependence and contempt.
locations.notes:
Despite the name, Touristville has genuine utility for runners. It is the most accessible point to acquire Barrens-sourced goods and services without going deep into gang territory. Fences, forgers, low-end ripperdocs, and information brokers all maintain presences here.

Lone Star does patrol Touristville — more than they patrol the actual Barrens — because the tourists represent a complaint demographic that has political pull. This makes it safer than most of Redmond but means the patrols are a factor to account for.

The Avalon is the most notable bar in Touristville — a British-themed establishment with pretensions to class that is undercut by its location and clientele. It serves as a middle-ground meeting spot for runners who find Club Penumbra too expensive and the Rusty Nail too deep in gang territory.

locations.name: The Avalon
locations.description: A British Isles-themed bar occupying a converted two-story building in Touristville. Dark wood paneling, Union Jack memorabilia, and a bartender who insists on being called 'the Publican' despite being a third-generation Seattle native. The Avalon positions itself as a cut above the surrounding Touristville establishments, which is a low bar it meets adequately.
locations.notes:
The Avalon functions as a middle-ground meeting spot — accessible from both the corporate side and the deep Barrens, neutral enough that most factions leave it alone, and cheap enough that runners without a current income can still afford a booth.

The Publican (real name: Gerald) knows everyone in Touristville and has strong opinions about all of them. He does not sell information directly but will steer people toward the right introductions if the tip is appropriate.

The upstairs function room can be rented by the hour for private meetings. It is not swept for surveillance devices. Assume it is monitored.

locations.name: Tarislar
locations.description: A predominantly elven community in the Puyallup district, established by elven refugees and economic migrants who were unwelcome in both the UCAS mainstream and the Tir Tairngire nation. Tarislar is politically active, culturally distinct, and deeply suspicious of outsiders — particularly humans, corps, and anyone associated with either Tir Tairngire's government or UCAS law enforcement. Despite its Barrens location, Tarislar maintains internal organization and community cohesion that most Barrens settlements lack entirely.
locations.notes:
Tarislar's community security is informal but effective — elven residents watch the neighborhood and respond to threats collectively. Outsiders who enter respectfully and have legitimate business are tolerated; outsiders who enter with aggression or corp affiliations will find the neighborhood extremely difficult to navigate and exit.

The community has complicated, contested feelings about Tir Tairngire. Some residents fled the Tir's rigid social hierarchy (the Danaan social rating system) and want nothing to do with the elven nation. Others maintain contact with Tir intelligence services or political factions. Runners who need a connection into Tir Tairngire shadow networks should start here — carefully.

Humanis Policlub has targeted Tarislar twice with arson and violence. Both times, Humanis operatives did not leave the neighborhood. This is not discussed openly.

The Ancients maintain a respectful distance from Tarislar — they recruit from the community but do not claim it as territory.

locations.name: Fort Lewis
locations.description: The primary UCAS Army installation in the Pacific Northwest, located south of the Seattle metroplex near the Tacoma border. Fort Lewis houses a full combined-arms brigade, aviation assets, military intelligence operations, and several restricted research programs. It is the largest concentration of conventional military force within several hundred kilometers of Seattle.
locations.notes:
Fort Lewis is not a runner target — it is the definition of a hard site. Physical security includes lethal perimeter defense, standing armed patrols, air assets, and a response force that can be combat-ready in under four minutes. Matrix security is maintained by dedicated military IC that operates under different rules than corporate hosts.

However, Fort Lewis is a source of military-grade equipment on the black market. Supply chain corruption exists at the NCO level — stolen milspec weapons, armor, and occasionally vehicles circulate through Seattle's shadow market with Fort Lewis provenance. The Mafia controls most of the established theft pipeline.

The installation houses a UCAS military intelligence detachment (designation classified) that occasionally runs deniable operations in Seattle using civilian assets. Runners who are hired for jobs that seem unusually well-resourced and vague about who the client is should consider whether the UCAS military is the actual employer.

Fort Lewis and Knight Errant have a formal mutual aid agreement for metropolitan defense scenarios. This means a major incident in Seattle could bring military assets into the city faster than most people expect.

locations.name: The Space Needle
locations.description: The pre-Awakening landmark that somehow survived Seattle's transformation into a sprawl. The Space Needle still stands at Seattle Center, now housing a high-end rotating restaurant at the observation level and a corporate event venue on the floors below. The view from the top — corporate towers, Renraku Arcology, the distant Barrens fires, and on clear days the volcanic plume from Puyallup — is the most complete single visual summary of what Seattle has become.
locations.notes:
The Space Needle restaurant is a legitimate high-end establishment. Dinner reservations run 200-400 nuyen per person and require a valid SIN. Corporate executives use it for client entertainment. The occasional Mr. Johnson meeting happens here precisely because it is so visible — nobody assumes the contact is shadow business when the setting is this conspicuous.

The observation deck is open to the public at 50 nuyen admission with SIN scan. It is a genuinely useful overwatch position for anyone planning operations in the Seattle Center area or mapping sightlines into the Downtown corporate district.

The building's age means its structural systems are pre-Matrix era — the security host is a bolted-on afterthought with limited integration. A decker looking to access the camera system will find it easier than most Downtown targets.

locations.name: Harborview Medical Center
locations.description: The largest public hospital in the Seattle metroplex and one of the few medical facilities that will treat patients regardless of SIN status — at a price. Harborview handles trauma, surgery, and emergency care for the portion of Seattle's population that cannot afford DocWagon contracts or corporate medical plans. The facility is perpetually understaffed and overextended, operating on public funding that has been cut four times in the last decade.
locations.notes:
SINless patients are accepted but the pricing structure is punishing. A SIN-verified patient pays standard rates (already high by Barrens standards). A SINless patient pays a 'risk surcharge' that typically runs 3-5x the base rate, justified by the hospital administration as covering the legal liability of treating an unregistered individual. In practice, runners who need legitimate trauma care without DocWagon involvement should budget 5,000-15,000 nuyen for anything serious, payable in certified credstick on admission.

Harborview's ER staff are experienced with gunshot wounds, blade trauma, and cyberware-related complications and ask far fewer questions than their corporate equivalents. Two of the attending trauma surgeons moonlight as informal ripperdocs for the shadow community and can be reached through Fingers.

The hospital maintains a Lone Star liaison office on the second floor. Officers respond to criminal incidents in the facility, which creates complications for runners seeking treatment after a high-profile job. The liaison office is understaffed and the officer on overnight shift is known to accept nuyen to delay filing incident reports.

DocWagon has a standing contract dispute with Harborview over patient acquisition — DocWagon claims the hospital is poaching their contract clients during emergencies. The dispute is ongoing and creates occasional friction between DocWagon HTR teams and Harborview admission staff.

locations.name: The Ork Underground
locations.description: A sprawling network of tunnels, converted utility spaces, and repurposed underground infrastructure beneath Downtown Seattle and the Pioneer Square district, inhabited primarily by orks and trolls who were pushed out of surface Seattle by economics, discrimination, and Humanis violence. The Ork Underground is not a single location but an entire subterranean community — with permanent residences, markets, workshops, and its own social hierarchy — that exists entirely below the surface city.
locations.notes:
The Underground is most prominently an SR3-era development, but its roots as an ork refuge are present in earlier Seattle lore. Use accordingly.

Lone Star has no jurisdiction underground — their charter covers the surface metroplex, and they have no formal enforcement presence in the tunnels. This makes the Underground a useful refuge for runners who need to disappear quickly after a Downtown operation.

Navigation without a guide is genuinely dangerous — the tunnel network is unmapped, partially flooded in sections, and patrolled by Underground security that does not extend courtesy to outsiders. A contact within the Underground community is necessary for safe transit.

The Crimson Crush has surface-level connections with Underground leadership — ork solidarity extends across the surface/underground divide — and can provide introductions for runners with appropriate standing.

characters.name: Razor
characters.description: Lean, covered in chrome. Favors katana and SMGs.
characters.background: Ex-Lone Star officer gone corporate. Burned his badge after a black op went wrong.
characters.notes: Owes a favor to Mr. Johnson at Ares. Don't bring it up.

reputation.notes: Known in Redmond circles as reliable muscle.

characters.title: Street Samurai

characters.name: Static
characters.description: Slight build, datajack behind the left ear, always has a deck nearby.
characters.background: Self-taught matrix runner from the Barrens. Never trusted corps.
characters.notes: Has a backdoor into the Lone Star dispatch grid. Valuable asset.

reputation.notes: Ghost on the grid. Hard to trace.

characters.title: Decker

characters.name: Fingers
characters.description: Fat, jovial, always eating. Missing the index finger on his left hand.
characters.background: Been in the shadows since the Awakening. Knows everyone, owes no one.
characters.notes: Primary fixer. Charges 10% on all job referrals.
characters.title: Fixer

characters.name: Marisol
characters.description: Unremarkable — deliberately so. Mid-level office attire, no corp logos, nothing that stands out. Nervous hands. Always picks a seat facing the exit.
characters.background: Works in Aztechnology Seattle's logistics division — senior enough to handle sealed shipping manifests, internal transfer schedules, and facility maintenance rosters. Not loyal enough to stay quiet when the nuyen is right. Has no idea what's in the crates she processes paperwork for, and has learned not to wonder.
characters.notes: Paranoid about being caught. Always meets off-campus, never at the same place twice. Payment in untraceable credsticks only. Useful for runners planning operations against Aztechnology sites who need timing or access intel without going in blind.
characters.title: Corporate Informant

characters.name: Santos
characters.description: Compact, well-dressed without being flashy. Always meets in public — cafes, markets, transit hubs. Speaks in careful misdirection that still communicates exactly what he means.
characters.background: Aztechnology's street-facing operator for deniable work in Seattle. Routes shadow contracts to runner teams without leaving corporate fingerprints. Knows just enough about the corp's darker programs to be useful — and careful. Has survived four handlers above him being quietly retired.
characters.notes: Pay is reliable and never late. Jobs escalate in sensitivity over time — runners who prove themselves get more complicated work. He doesn't ask questions and expects the same courtesy. If you burn him once, you won't get a second meeting.
characters.title: Deniable Ops Handler

characters.name: Sister Ixtli
characters.description: Unhurried and deliberate. Wears a corp badge tucked under a hand-woven rebozo in Aztlan colors. Eyes that track things that aren't there — because for her, they are.
characters.background: Manages the spiritual security of Aztechnology's Seattle campus: wards, anchored spirits, astral perimeter maintenance. Not a researcher — she's the person who ensures nothing enters or exits astrally without clearance. Deeply spiritual, a true believer in Aztlan's magical tradition. Will deal with Awakened runners who demonstrate genuine respect for the tradition, provided they don't ask her to work against the corp.
characters.notes: Will not assist in anything that directly targets Aztechnology. Awakened runners who approach with respect for Aztlan tradition will find her more forthcoming than expected. Particularly useful for runners navigating magically-secured sites — she knows what wards and spirits are deployed in Seattle and can advise without revealing corp-proprietary specifics.
characters.title: Ritual Site Warden

characters.name: J0k3r
characters.description: Wiry and manic, face painted in a permanent clown smile with burn scars underneath that tell a different story. Wears a patchwork Halloween costume jacket over ballistic mesh. Always smells faintly of accelerant.
characters.background: Grew up in the Redmond Barrens and found the Halloweeners at fifteen — not for protection, but because he liked fire. Made himself indispensable as the gang's go-to for anything that needs to burn. The handle comes from a datajack tattoo behind his ear and a habit of texting targets a burning clown emoji before a job. Connected enough to know who's selling what in Redmond; paranoid enough to still be alive.
characters.notes: Useful for runners operating in Redmond who need local street knowledge, untraceable incendiary materials, or a distraction that involves something burning. Unreliable under pressure — loyalty is to the gang and to chaos, in that order. Do not invite him to meetings in buildings you like.
characters.title: Halloweener Enforcer

characters.name: Victor Graves
characters.description: Broad-shouldered, expensive suit, handshake like a vice. Smiles easily. The smile never fully reaches his eyes.
characters.background: Runs deniable operations out of the Ares Seattle tower. Routes shadow contracts through cutouts so clean they're almost art. Razor owes him a favor from a black op gone sideways — Graves pulled strings to make the incident disappear. He hasn't called it in yet.
characters.notes: Razor owes him a favor. He hasn't called it in yet. When he does, it won't be optional. Pay is excellent; job parameters are flexible; expectations are absolute. Runners who deliver cleanly get repeat business. Those who talk don't.
characters.title: Corporate Mr. Johnson

characters.name: Hana Mori
characters.description: Sharp, quiet, always slightly distracted — like part of her attention is somewhere else entirely. Usually is. Wears a datajack she doesn't hide.
characters.background: Assigned to SCIRE Arcology network infrastructure. Senior enough to know the architecture of the lower system levels. Stopped asking questions about what runs there two years ago. The questions stopped on their own after that.
characters.notes: Will cooperate if approached carefully with evidence she's not being monitored. Afraid of the lower Arcology levels — will not discuss them directly, but has been known to leave files in accessible locations. The things she knows about the SCIRE's subsystems are not things she chose to learn.
characters.title: Corporate Decker

characters.name: Ernst Kessler
characters.description: Formal to the point of rigidity. Impeccable suit, perfect posture, records every meeting. Has been in Seattle for twelve years and shows no sign of leaving.
characters.background: Lofwyr's eyes and voice in Seattle. Conveys decisions rather than making them — a distinction he maintains with great precision. There is a standing rumor that he is under Lofwyr's direct personal surveillance at all times. He neither confirms nor denies it.
characters.notes: Pay for SK contract work is exceptional and always accurate. Each engagement's demands increase incrementally. Whether this is standard SK practice or something more deliberate is unclear. Runners who work for him twice tend to work for him a third time.
characters.title: Corporate Representative

characters.name: Lt. Sonya Vasquez
characters.description: Compact, alert, tactical armor always within reach. Commands without raising her voice. Holds genuine contempt for Lone Star's institutional corruption — doesn't hide it.
characters.background: Commands Knight Errant's Bellevue corporate sector rapid response unit. Has passed documented misconduct intel on Lone Star to Ares oversight — twice. Loathes Hargrove specifically. Will cooperate with runners when it serves KE or Ares interests, provided they stay out of her jurisdiction.
characters.notes: A useful ally when runner goals align with KE or Ares interests. Despises Hargrove personally — mentioning him tends to open doors. Do not operate in Bellevue's corporate sector without her knowledge; she will find out and she will remember.
characters.title: Field Commander

characters.name: Tanaka Kenji
characters.description: Unhurried and precise. Missing the tip of his left pinky — the cut was his own choice, years ago. Speaks quietly. Everyone in the room listens.
characters.background: Effectively runs the Watada-rengo's day-to-day Seattle operations. The arrangement with Hargrove goes through his desk. Has survived three oyabun transitions by being the man no one could afford to replace.
characters.notes: Runners who deliver cleanly will be received professionally and paid accurately. Those who fail, talk, or embarrass the organization will not be received again — in any sense of the word. The Hargrove arrangement is not something he discusses.
characters.title: Yakuza Underboss

characters.name: Tommy "Two-Shoes" Marconi
characters.description: Big, loud, third-generation Seattle Italian. Runs his operation out of a waterfront restaurant he legitimately owns. The food is excellent. The back room is not a restaurant.
characters.background: Controls the Tacoma docks operation — cargo movement, union intimidation, loan-sharking across the south end. Not subtle about any of it. Maintains an uneasy territorial truce with the Yakuza that holds as long as no one tests it.
characters.notes: Useful for moving freight through the port without inspection. Do not simultaneously work for him and the Yakuza — word gets around and he takes it personally. Not subtle; not patient; entirely reliable as long as you treat him with appropriate respect.
characters.title: Mafia Capo

characters.name: Red Phoenix
characters.description: Hong Kong-born, moves like she's always half a second ahead of the room. Red Dragon tattoo on her right forearm — visible, not hidden. Manages the Association's Chinatown and Downtown gambling interests.
characters.background: Handles both enforcement and negotiation for the Red Dragon Association's Seattle operations. The combination is deliberate — she closes deals or she closes people, depending on what the situation requires. Maintains strict professional standards and expects them in return.
characters.notes: Meet the Association's professional standards or don't contact her. Will not deal with anyone associated with the Seoulpa Rings — treat this as a hard constraint, not a preference. Pacific Rim smuggling routes she controls are not available through any other Seattle contact.
characters.title: Triad Enforcer

characters.name: Ghost
characters.description: Doesn't use her real name professionally — Park Ji-Young is for paperwork she makes herself. Operates out of the International District. Always knows two exits.
characters.background: Specializes in moving people and goods without documentation. Survives by maintaining careful neutrality in the ongoing Yakuza/Triad territorial conflict — she works around it, not inside it. Has kept the same comm code for six years, which is a long time in her line of work.
characters.notes: Highly reliable as long as you don't ask about her other clients. Charges a premium for urgency. Has no interest in the Yakuza/Triad war and will drop any client who tries to pull her into it.
characters.title: Smuggler

characters.name: "Blade" Kowalski
characters.description: Scarred, wiry, always has a blade within reach and usually two he hasn't mentioned. Grew up three blocks from Razor. Treats that history as currency.
characters.background: Works the Puyallup/Barrens border for the Cutters. Razor's standing contact within the gang — they came up in the same neighborhood, which counts for something in both directions. Has held the lieutenant position for four years by being the one who shows up when things go wrong.
characters.notes: Razor's in with the Cutters runs through Kowalski. Transactional but reliable — he delivers what he promises and expects the same. Can arrange short-notice safe houses south of the Barrens. Don't bring him into anything that crosses the Cutters' interests.
characters.title: Gang Lieutenant

characters.name: Boss Crushbone
characters.description: Built like a structural wall, moves like he knows it. Born in the Ork Underground. Speaks slowly and carefully — learned early that orks who talk fast get ignored.
characters.background: Leads the Crimson Crush with deep roots in both Redmond and the Underground community. Deeply loyal to the ork community across surface and underground — ork solidarity is not rhetoric to him. Has kept the Crush viable through two turf wars by knowing when to fight and when to wait.
characters.notes: Will not deal with runners who treat orks as tools or beneath consideration. Those who show genuine respect will find him reliable for street intel and no-questions-asked safe harbor. Hostile relationship with J0k3r and the Halloweeners — the Crush and Halloweeners are territorial enemies in Redmond. Can provide introductions to Underground leadership for runners who earn it.
characters.title: Gang Leader

characters.name: Silverstring
characters.description: Appears mid-twenties. Has looked that way for a century. Silver hair worn loose, eyes that have seen more Seattle than the city's current buildings. Monofilament sword carried like it belongs there.
characters.background: One of the Seattle chapter's founding members. Survived four gang wars. Practices a self-taught shamanic tradition she rarely discusses. Maintains the Ancients' elves-only membership rule without apology or explanation. Deals with non-elf runners only when she judges it genuinely necessary, on terms she sets.
characters.notes: Elvish PCs receive considerably warmer treatment. The Ancients' Tir Tairngire trade routes are not available through any other Seattle contact. Non-elf runners who approach without proper introduction should expect to be turned away the first time.
characters.title: Gang Captain

characters.name: Councilman Derek Walsh
characters.description: Well-dressed, silver-tongued, always on message. Bellevue city council member. Humanis's most visible elected face in Seattle. Believes his own rhetoric — which makes him considerably more dangerous than the cynical operatives around him.
characters.background: Publicly advocates metahuman accountability legislation. Privately funds street-level intimidation campaigns and provides Humanis with political cover. Has survived two corruption investigations through community support and careful paperwork.
characters.notes: Useful only for runners willing to work alongside a genuine ideologue, or for those running operations against Humanis. Metahuman runners who meet with him should assume the meeting will be reported to Humanis street operators if anything goes wrong. He is not performing — he means it.
characters.title: Political Operative

characters.name: Chief Hargrove
characters.description: Stocky, silver-haired, perpetually scowling. Twenty years in Lone Star. Knows where every body is buried — including several, literally.
characters.background: Rose through Lone Star's ranks during the corp's prime. Now managing decline while keeping his own hands clean enough. Maintains quiet relationships with organized crime that have long since compromised his integrity. Razor knows enough to be a problem. He tips Razor off occasionally in exchange for silence, but the arrangement is entirely transactional.
characters.notes: Has standing arrangements with the Yakuza's Watada-rengo. His private node (LST-DIR1) contains enough to end careers — including his own. Razor worked under him briefly and knows too much for Hargrove's comfort.
characters.title: Lone Star Director

contacts.notes: Fingers got Razor his first job out of Lone Star. Solid relationship.
Static doesn't fully trust fixers but Fingers delivers.
Former boss. Tense. Will tip off Razor occasionally in exchange for keeping quiet.

org_standings.notes: Done a few jobs for them.
Hacked their grid once. They don't know it was her.

adventure_logs.title: The Aztechnology Extraction
adventure_logs.objective: Extract Dr. Yolanda Vega from Aztechnology Research Campus before she's transferred.
adventure_logs.result: Dr. Vega extracted successfully. Static took a light wound. Facility placed on alert.
adventure_logs.casualties: Two Aztechnology security guards KO'd. No runner deaths.
adventure_logs.gm_notes: Aztechnology will be looking for the runners. Raise heat level next session. Tezca has been briefed.
